title: Ordinal Distance-To-Next-Stop Sidecar
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop decision work uses both next-lap classification and remaining-laps-to-stop regression; discrete-time survival framing also treats event timing as interval hazards.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop decision work uses both next-lap classification and remaining-laps-to-stop regression; discrete-time survival framing also treats event timing as interval hazards. This uses more supervision than the binary PitNextLap label alone.
implementation_hint: From train sequences, compute laps_until_next_pit for each Race_Year/Driver/Stint. Train an ordinal/regression sidecar for bins like 0, 1, 2, 3-5, 6+, convert P(distance<=1) via OOF isotonic mapping, and stack that score into the main model.
expected_effect: Uses near-miss laps as graded signal, smoothing sparse positives and improving next-lap ranking.
risk: Race-end censoring and malformed sequences can inject noise; censor final stints explicitly.
